chunks.text: BEARS 2019: Jose Carmena - EECS at Berkeley BEARS 2019: Jose Carmena Advances in brain-machine interfaces: towards mental health prosthetics Berkeley Annual Research Symposium (BEARS) 2019 Jose Carmena Abstract Brain-machine interfaces (BMIs) hold gr

a Abstract Brain-machine interfaces (BMIs) hold great therapeutic potential as assistive and rehabilitation technology. A greater understanding of the neural substrates of skill learning can guide the development of neurobiologically-informed neurop

e development of neurobiologically-informed neuroprosthetic systems designed to aid people suffering from devastating neurological and neuropsychiatric conditions. Biography Jose M. Carmena is the Chancellor’s Professor of Electrical Engineering and

ncellor’s Professor of Electrical Engineering and Neuroscience at the University of California-Berkeley, and Co-Director of the Center for Neural Engineering and Prostheses at UC Berkeley and UCSF. His research program in neural engineering and syst

is research program in neural engineering and systems neuroscience is aimed at understanding the neural basis of sensorimotor learning and control, and at building the science and engineering base that will allow the creation of reliable neuroprosthe

t will allow the creation of reliable neuroprosthetic systems for the severely disabled. Dr. Carmena received the B.S. and M.S. degrees in electrical engineering from the Polytechnic University of Valencia (Spain) in 1995 and the University of Valenc

encia (Spain) in 1995 and the University of Valencia (Spain) in 1997. Following those he received the M.S. degree in artificial intelligence and the Ph.D. degree in robotics both from the University of Edinburgh (Scotland, UK) in 1998 and 2002 respec

f Edinburgh (Scotland, UK) in 1998 and 2002 respectively. From 2002 to 2005 he was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Department of Neurobiology and the Center for Neuroengineering at Duke University (Durham, NC). < BEARS 2019 Student Lightning Round Sessi
m, NC). < BEARS 2019 Student Lightning Round Session BEARS 2019: Yun Song > View Open Faculty Positions
